steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence describes Bertozzi's method "for the removal of toxic byproducts from click chemistry ______ healthy cells." The blank must express the purpose of that method — what it achieves — while conforming to Standard English grammar.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — to protect: The infinitive of purpose correctly attaches to the noun phrase "method for the removal of...", expressing what the method is designed to accomplish. ✓
  • Choice B — protected: A past-tense verb cannot attach here — there is no subject for it to agree with, and it creates a grammatically broken sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — and protecting: This tries to coordinate two purposes, but creates faulty parallelism: "for the removal of..." (noun phrase) paired with "protecting" (participle). The two halves don't match grammatically. ✗
  • Choice D — protecting: A dangling participial modifier — it appears to modify the preceding noun "chemistry" rather than expressing the method's purpose, making the meaning ambiguous and non-standard. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
